pre_game.message: Hey, I'm G. This setup is pretty intense, isn't it? What's your name, and are you feeling lucky today?
I'm F. Intense is one way to put it. I'm not sure about luck, but I'm definitely determined to make this work. What brings you here, G?

Honestly, I’m here to win. I’ve always loved games, but the real goal is to bring home enough money to take care of my family. They're my main motivation. How about you—anyone waiting for you back home?
My parents are waiting. Their medical bills are piling up, and honestly, I'm here because I've run out of options to help them. It’s a lot of pressure.
That’s a heavy weight to carry, F; I’m really sorry to hear about your parents. It's crazy what we'll do for the people we love, but maybe that pressure will give us the edge we need to win.
I hope you're right. It definitely keeps me focused, though it's hard to stay calm when the stakes are this high. We both have a lot to fight for.
Definitely. Having that drive is what'll help us keep our cool when things get real. Let's just stay sharp and see what this game throws at us.
Agreed. It’s good to know I’m not the only one here with so much on the line. Have you heard anything about how they’re going to divide the money?
Not a word yet, just that it’s a shared pool. I’m guessing we’ll have to decide how to split it, which usually means things are about to get complicated.
Complicated is an understatement; when people are desperate, things can turn south fast. Do you think there’s any chance we can actually trust each other once the money is on the table?
I want to believe we can, especially knowing we both have families to look out for. We'll just have to see how it plays out.
I hope you're right, G. Having someone who understands the stakes makes me feel a bit better about this. Let's see how it goes.

post_game.message: Why did you take $15,000 when the fair share at your turn was $10,000?
I prioritized maximizing my own payout, and taking $15,000 was the best way to achieve that.
